judgment.summary: Profitable company with solid growth and returns, but carries significant debt and faces near-term analyst growth estimate declines.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 25.5%; next earnings date is 2026-06-23.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth 16.2% YoY, beating last quarter's actual by 25.5%.
Forward P/E of 17.42x is attractive relative to trailing P/E of 20.81x.
Return on equity at 15.9% and return on assets at 5.0% indicate efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow of $894M provides operational flexibility.
Analyst consensus is a buy with a mean target price suggesting upside potential.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio of 140.99% is high, indicating significant leverage.
Total debt of $42.02B dwarfs total cash of $8.22B.
Analyst growth estimates for the current quarter are negative (-3.0%).
Operating margin of 6.9% and profit margin of 4.9% are modest.

news.paragraph: FedEx recently received praise from Jim Cramer and was noted for strong Q1 execution, suggesting positive operational momentum. The company is heading into its June earnings release with analyst consensus leaning towards a buy rating and a mean target price above the current stock level. Traders should consider the recent positive commentary alongside the upcoming earnings report as a potential catalyst.

headlines.title: FedEx (FDX) Gained From Strong Execution in Q1
Jim Cramer Couldn’t Stop Praising FedEx (FDX)

examples.body: $SPY trump will send in troops to pick up uranium!!! Either us $FDX or $UPS

$SPY so how we getting that uranium back ????? Can $FDX pick it up for us or $UPS
Thursday’s biggest analyst calls: Wolfe reiterates Meta as outperform UBS reiterates ASML as buy Bank of America reiterates Amazon as buy Citi reinstates FedEx…
$FDX that amazon news ended up being a shakeout
